subject: Cloud Computing
title: Hypervisors: The Hidden Layer
description: One-minute skill: Hypervisors: The Hidden Layer.

hook.text: Between your operating system and the hardware, there's a hidden layer doing the real work.

buildup.text: A hypervisor creates and manages virtual machines on physical hardware.

discovery.text: Type 1 runs directly on hardware. Type 2 runs on top of an existing operating system.

twist.text: Cloud providers use Type 1 — but they've built custom versions far beyond anything off-the-shelf.

climax.text: AWS built Nitro, its own hypervisor. Google uses custom KVM. Each guards its design as a secret.

punchline.text: The hypervisor is the cloud's invisible foundation.

quiz.question: What does a hypervisor do?
options: Creates and manages virtual machines on physical hardware
Connects data centers to the internet
Encrypts data stored in the cloud
